chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 799 of 2020 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of CMA No.3671/2020 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 21.02.2024 Syed Zafar Ali Shah Bukhari, Advocate holding brief for Mr. Bakhshan Khan Mahar, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant Advocate General Sindh..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Through this petition, petitioner is seeking directions to respondents to appoint him on the basis of deceased quota as Sub-Engineer or Security Officer. On the last date of hearing, learned Counsel for petitioner was put on notice to satisfy as to whether Rule 11-A of the Sindh Civil Servants (Appointment, Promotion and Transfer) Rules, 1974, is applicable in this case as admittedly petitioner’s father was not a civil servant. Today, he has chosen to remain absent and on his behalf brief is held by another Counsel. Learned AAG Sindh submits that the very rule is not applicable in the case of petitioner as his father was not a civil servant being employed in Sindh Industrial Trading Estates Limited. Faced with this situation, learned Counsel holding brief submits that he would be satisfied if this petition is disposed of by directing the respondents to consider the case of petitioner on humanitarian basis. This request appears to be reasonable, and this petition is accordingly disposed of along with listed application. Respondents may consider the case of petitioner for appointment on humanitarian ground. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
